channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: Blinken heads to Asia, Europe on last expected trip as top US diplomat
item.description: Secretary of State Antony Blinken will embark on what is expected to be his final overseas trip in office this weekend, traveling to South Korea, Japan and France.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: The French Quarter's metal barriers were gone on New Year's, leaving a critical security gap
item.description: The city of New Orleans tried to protect its famed French Quarter from a terrorist attack years before a U.S. Army veteran rammed a truck into crowds of New Year's revelers on Bourbon Street, killing 14 people.
item.dc:creator: Ryan Foley And Michael Kunzelman, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Greenland leader calls for independence from Denmark as Trump enters
item.description: Greenland Prime Minister Múte Egede announced a desire to obtain independence from Denmark in the wake of President-elect Trump’s comments about buying the island territory.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, The Hill

item.title: Bipartisan lawmakers renew push for legislation supporting Georgia against Russian interference
item.description: Republican and Democratic lawmakers are renewing efforts to pass legislation supporting Georgia, touting it as one of the best and first examples of bipartisan unity of the new Congress. Reps. Joe Wilson (R-S.C.) and Steve Cohen (D-Tenn.
item.dc:creator: Laura Kelly, The Hill

item.title: Montenegro to tackle gun control after mass killing left 12 dead
item.description: PODGORICA, Montenegro (AP) — A top-level meeting in Montenegro on Friday promised tough measures to curb illegal weapons after a gunman fatally shot 12 people in a second such tragedy in less than three years in the small Balkan country.
item.dc:creator: PREDRAG MILIC, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: 1 cigarette can decrease life expectancy by 20 minutes: Data
item.description: In 2000, data published in The BMJ estimated one cigarette would cost a person 11 minutes of their life.
item.dc:creator: Patrick Djordjevic, The Hill

item.title: Middle East latest: Germany and France seek a 'new beginning' with Syria
item.description: The top diplomats from Germany and France were in Syria on Friday to send what the German minister called a clear signal that Europe and Syria can have a “political new beginning" after Islamist insurgents ousted Bashar Assad.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Jake Sullivan, Biden discussed possibility of hitting Iran nuclear program: report
item.description: President Biden was presented scenario and response options on how to handle Iran should it make advances in developing a nuclear weapon, though the president has not signed off on hitting Tehran's program.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: One cigarette can decrease life expectancy by 20 minutes: Data
item.description: In 2000, data published in The BMJ estimated one cigarette would cost a person 11 minutes of their life.
item.dc:creator: Patrick Djordjevic, NewsNation

item.title: Zelenskyy says Trump could be ‘decisive’ in bringing an end to the war
item.description: Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy said he thinks President-elect Donald Trump could be "decisive" in ending the nearly three-years-long war and dealing with Russian President Vladimir Putin's demands.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Investigators meet in Brazil to extract data from black box of crashed Azerbaijani airliner
item.description: RIO DE JANEIRO (AP) — Several of Brazilian air force investigators are working with colleagues from three other nations to analyze data from the Azerbaijani airliner that crashed in Kazakhstan on Dec. 25, killing 38 people. Azerbaijan claims the jet was unintentionally shot down by Russia.
item.dc:creator: MAURICIO SAVARESE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump blasts UK energy policy and ‘windmills’ after company blames windfall tax for less drilling
item.description: President-elect Trump is criticizing the United Kingdom’s energy policy and saying it should get rid of windmills after a company blamed the country’s windfall tax for its early exit from drilling in Europe’s North Sea. In a post on his social media site Truth Social, Trump wrote, “The U.K.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Frazin, The Hill

item.title: At least 30 people killed by Israeli strikes in Gaza
item.description: At least 30 people, including children, were killed in Gaza by Israeli strikes overnight and into Friday morning.
item.dc:creator: WAFAA SHURAFA and SAM MEDNICK, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: South Korea's impeached president defies warrant after hourslong standoff
item.description: SEOUL, South Korea (AP) — South Korean investigators left the official residence of impeached President Yoon Suk Yeol after a near-six-hour standoff on Friday during which he defied their attempt to detain him.
item.dc:creator: KIM TONG-HYUNG, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Iran executes over 1K prisoners in 2024, highest total in 30 years, report says
item.description: A new report claimed Iran executed an unprecedented number of people in 2024. Most of the executions took place following the election of its new president in late July.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump's strength and unpredictability can help end the war in Ukraine, Zelenskyy says
item.description: Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy says U.S. President-elect Donald Trump is “strong and unpredictable” and those qualities can be a decisive factor in his policy approach to Russia’s invasion of Ukraine.
item.dc:creator: Illia Novikov, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: South Korea’s impeached president avoids arrest attempt after hourslong standoff
item.description: Impeached South Korean President Yoon Suk Yeol avoided an arrest attempt Friday after an hourslong standoff between anti-corruption investigators and presidential security.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Ukraine halts transit of Russian gas to Europe after a prewar deal expired
item.description: KYIV, Ukraine (AP) — Ukraine on Wednesday halted Russian gas supplies to European customers through its pipeline network after a prewar transit deal expired at the end of 2024 and almost three years into Moscow's all-out invasion of its neighbor.
item.dc:creator: HANNA ARHIROVA and JOANNA KOZLOWSKA, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: The world welcomes 2025 with light shows, embraces and ice plunges
item.description: The New Year’s Eve ball dropped in soggy Times Square, where thousands of revelers stuck it out in heavy rain to celebrate.
item.dc:creator: MELISSA GOLDIN, CEDAR ATTANASIO and CHARLOTTE GRAHAM-McLAY, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: At least 66 people die after a truck plunges into a river in southern Ethiopia
item.description: At least 66 people were killed in southern Ethiopia after an overcrowded truck that was contracted by wedding guests plunged into a river.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Berlin police say they have detained a man who attacked 2 people with a knife
item.description: BERLIN (AP) — Berlin police said they detained a man Tuesday who attacked and injured two people in the city's Charlottenburg neighborhood, further jolting Germany days after a deadly Christmas market attack elsewhere in the country.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Putin offers to pay off debts as recruitment tool in war against Ukraine
item.description: Russian President Vladimir Putin signed a bill into law last month that offers to pay off up to $96,000 in debt in exchange for military service.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Israeli spy network uncovers Hezbollah commander's plans to marry his 4 mistresses
item.description: Israeli spy agencies infiltrated Hezbollah so successfully that they learned intimate details of commander Fuad Shukr's plans to marry his four mistresses.
item.dc:creator: Fox News
